title: Non-2xx responses use the WSDenatran error envelope
statement: Every non-2xx response body is exactly `{ returnCode: integer, message: string }`, produced by a single global exception filter — never a raw string or a NestJS default error shape. Status semantics follow the contract: 400 requisição inválida, 401 não autorizado, 402 erro de negócio, 404 não encontrado, 500 erro interno. `returnCode` equals the HTTP status.
rationale: The official contract specifies this envelope for all error codes. Client error handling in pec/teat depends on it being uniform and stable across all 57 endpoints; a leaked framework error shape or a bare string would break consumers.
